description: Instrument family spectral fingerprints. Used to estimate which instrument families are present from frequency and temporal patterns.

kick_drum.name: Kick Drum
kick_drum.psychosomatic_note: Sub-bass kick entrains the body's autonomic systems. At 120-140 BPM it approaches resting heart rate acceleration zone. The four-on-the-floor pattern is the most widely deployed body-priming pattern in recorded music.
kick_drum.manufacturing_indicator: Perfectly grid-aligned kick at 120-140 BPM with sub-bass extension is a body priming signature. Context determines whether this is communal or extractive.

snare_drum.name: Snare Drum
snare_drum.gated_reverb_signature: 1980s signature: sharp attack, zero sustain, brief noise burst
snare_drum.psychosomatic_note: Snare placement relative to the grid is one of the primary groove deviation markers. Human snare lands slightly behind the beat (pocket). Machine snare is exactly on grid.

hi_hat.name: Hi-Hat
hi_hat.psychosomatic_note: Hi-hat patterns carry the micro-rhythmic information of groove. Trap hi-hats create a particular nervous-system response — rapid irregular stimulation in the high frequencies.

bass_guitar.name: Bass Guitar
bass_guitar.psychosomatic_note: The low-frequency anchor. Felt in the chest and abdomen before it is heard. Bass presence is the most body-primary instrument family.

electric_guitar.name: Electric Guitar
electric_guitar.distortion_signature: harmonic saturation above 2kHz, even harmonics = warmth, odd = harshness
electric_guitar.psychosomatic_note: Distorted guitar's odd-harmonic content creates perceived bite and aggression. The same physical sensation as raised voice.

acoustic_guitar.name: Acoustic Guitar
acoustic_guitar.authenticity_marker: string noise, fret noise, body tap audible
acoustic_guitar.psychosomatic_note: Acoustic guitar's presence in recording carries physical reality markers (string noise, body movement). Their presence is an authenticity indicator — they weren't cleaned out.

piano.name: Piano / Keys
piano.psychosomatic_note: Piano's wide frequency range and harmonic complexity creates the most information-dense tonal source. Piano-led harmonic structure indicates deliberate compositional authorship.

synthesiser.name: Synthesiser

era_signatures.early_analogue_1970s: warm, slightly unstable oscillators, filter sweep artifacts
era_signatures.digital_1980s: clean, bright, static timbre, FM artifacts
era_signatures.modular_contemporary: complex, evolving, often harsh or unusual timbres

synthesiser.psychosomatic_note: Synthesiser timbres carry era signatures strongly. The specific artifacts of vintage analogue (filter resonance, oscillator instability) vs clean digital creates different somatic responses — warmth vs precision.

voice.name: Human Voice
voice.psychosomatic_note: The voice is the most honest instrument. It carries the autonomic state of the body that produced it — laryngeal tension, breath holding, suppressed vibrato. These are signals the voice cannot fully conceal.

drums_acoustic.name: Acoustic Drums
drums_acoustic.room_character: room acoustics audible in overheads and room mics
drums_acoustic.psychosomatic_note: Acoustic drums carry the physical presence of a body in space. The room acoustics mean the listener's body is placed in the same acoustic environment as the performer.

drum_machine.name: Drum Machine

characteristic_models.TR_808: heavy sub-bass kick decay, rimshot, cowbell
characteristic_models.TR_909: punchy kick, sharp snare, metallic hi-hats

drum_machine.psychosomatic_note: Drum machine timing is intentional — it reflects a production choice, not a physical limit. Context determines whether machine-precise rhythm serves the music or removes the body's rhythm from it.

strings.name: Strings / Orchestral
strings.psychosomatic_note: Strings create sympathetic resonance responses. The human auditory system has ancestral recognition of bowed instruments — associated with human vocal emotion. Orchestral strings in commercial production are a prestige signal deployed to elevate manufactured content.

horns_brass.name: Horns / Brass
horns_brass.psychosomatic_note: Brass section presence usually indicates ensemble recording — multiple humans in a room. This creates natural dynamic covariance. Also carries cultural markers of collective celebration and community.

sub_bass_electronic.name: Sub-Bass Electronic
sub_bass_electronic.psychosomatic_note: Sub-bass below 60Hz is felt more than heard — primarily a physical experience. This is the most direct body-entrainment tool in modern production. Heavy sub-bass (>-20dB in the 20-60Hz range) is a direct somatic intervention.
sub_bass_electronic.priming_indicator: sub_bass_energy_ratio > 0.3 with four_on_the_floor kick = body priming signature
